source_jobs.title: Vice President of Government & Public Sector Sales
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.

We are seeking a Vice President of Government & Public Sector Sales to lead and scale Strider’s public sector business across federal, state, and local government. This executive leader will define and execute our government go-to-market strategy, build a world-class sales organization, and establish the operational rigor, customer relationships, and cross-functional partnerships required to accelerate long-term growth. Working across Sales, Product, Marketing, Engineering, and Customer Success, you will help government organizations leverage strategic intelligence to address mission-critical challenges while expanding Strider’s impact across the public sector.




DESCRIPTION

 - Develop and execute Strider’s government go-to-market strategy across federal, state, and local markets, including direct sales, strategic partnerships, government contract vehicles, and channel development.

 - Establish and lead the operating rhythm for the Government business, driving consistent execution through disciplined pipeline reviews, forecasting, inspection, account planning, and performance management.

 - Build, mentor, and scale a high-performing team of Government Account Executives and Capture Managers while fostering a culture of accountability, urgency, collaboration, and high-velocity execution.

 - Partner closely with Business Development Representatives (BDRs) and Marketing to develop and execute pipeline generation strategies, targeted campaigns, and account-based programs that expand market awareness and accelerate qualified pipeline growth.

 - Build trusted executive relationships with agency stakeholders, including procurement leaders, mission owners, CIO organizations, and intelligence community partners, serving as a strategic advisor throughout the acquisition lifecycle.

 - Lead complex government pursuits, including RFIs, RFPs, capture strategies, and contract vehicle opportunities, ensuring competitive positioning and compliance throughout the procurement process.

 - Partner with Forward Deployed Engineering, Solution Engineering, Customer Success, and post-sales teams to deliver measurable customer value, accelerate adoption, and identify opportunities to expand existing government relationships.

 - Serve as the voice of the government market by bringing customer insights, competitive intelligence, and go-to-market priorities into Product, Marketing, and executive planning to influence roadmap decisions and market strategy.

 - Collaborate with Legal, Security, and Compliance teams to navigate procurement requirements, security reviews, FedRAMP initiatives, and authorization processes.

 - Partner with Marketing and Solution Engineering to develop compelling executive briefings, demonstrations, proposals, and messaging tailored to government mission needs.




KEY QUALIFICATIONS


MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 15+ years of enterprise sales leadership experience with a proven track record selling technology solutions to federal, state, and/or local government agencies.

 - Demonstrated success building and scaling government technology businesses from approximately $10M–$50M in annual revenue to $100M+ through a combination of direct sales, channel partnerships, and capture excellence.

 - Deep expertise navigating the government acquisition lifecycle, including GSA Schedules, IDIQs, GWACs, BPAs, OASIS, and other federal contract vehicles.

 - Established executive relationships with senior government stakeholders, including procurement officials, mission owners, CIO organizations, defense agencies, and the Intelligence Community.

 - Proven ability to recruit, develop, a

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.

Strider is seeking a mission-driven Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) to deliver cutting-edge technical solutions in dynamic, high-stakes environments. As a key member of our deployment team, you’ll bridge the gap between our advanced platform and real-world mission requirements—embedding with customers, engineering bespoke solutions, and serving as a trusted technical partner. This is a unique opportunity to directly impact national security and commercial innovation while helping shape Strider’s future product development. If you thrive on building in the field, solving critical problems, and working side-by-side with analysts and operators, we want to hear from you. 

 

Key Qualifications 

 - 3+ years of professional software engineering experience, with a focus on full-stack or systems-level development. 

 - Proficiency in Python, Java, or similar languages. 

 - Experience with distributed systems, cloud infrastructure (AWS preferred), APIs, and modern front-end frameworks. 

 - Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to rapidly prototype, iterate, and deploy in fast-paced environments. 

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to engage both technical and non-technical stakeholders. 

 - Demonstrated ability to operate effectively in ambiguous or evolving mission contexts. 

 - Willingness to travel 30–70% of the time based on mission requirements. 

 - Active Level 3 security clearance or eligibility to obtain one strongly preferred. 

 

Description 

As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you will be on the front lines of technical delivery—partnering directly with customers to tailor Strider’s platform to their specific mission needs. Your role will span engineering execution and mission integration, requiring technical depth, situational awareness, and operational agility. 

You will: 

 - Architect, develop, and deploy mission-aligned software solutions in government and commercial environments. 

 - Collaborate with operators, analysts, and executives to translate evolving requirements into actionable technical outcomes. 

 - Integrate Strider’s platform with diverse data sources, internal workflows, and external IT systems. 

 - Build custom applications, features, and data pipelines that unlock insights from unstructured, multi-lingual datasets. 

 - Serve as Strider’s technical lead in the field, owning project outcomes from inception to implementation. 

 - Prototype quickly and iterate continuously, adapting to high-priority demands and real-time feedback. 

 - Provide critical field insights to inform and influence Strider’s internal product roadmap. 

 

Why Join Strider? 

 - Be a key contributor to a high-impact mission that directly supports innovation and national resilience. 

 - Join a collaborative, fast-paced team that values creativity, autonomy, and trust. 

 - Work across domains—engineering, intelligence, product—on some of today’s most complex challenges. 

 - Develop leadership skills at the intersection of technology and mission. 

 

Benefits 

 - Competitive Compensation 

 - Company Equity Options 

 - Flexible PTO 

 - Wellness Reimbursement 

 - US Holidays (Office Closed) 

 - Paid Parental Leave 

 - Comprehensive Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance 

 - 401(k) Plan 

Strider is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace and do not discriminate against employees or applicants based on race, color, religion, gender, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We comply with all relevant employment la

source_jobs.title: Senior Client Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.

As a Senior Client Success Manager, you will play a critical role in helping some of the world’s largest organizations maximize the value of their partnership with Strider. You will own strategic relationships with enterprise clients, guiding them from onboarding through renewal while helping them achieve measurable business outcomes. Beyond managing a portfolio of accounts, you will contribute to the continued growth of Strider’s Client Success organization by helping refine processes, share best practices, and shape the future of the customer experience.

This role is ideal for an experienced enterprise Client Success professional who enjoys building executive relationships, solving complex business challenges, and partnering across teams to deliver exceptional client outcomes.




KEY QUALIFICATIONS


MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of Customer Success experience supporting strategic Enterprise SaaS clients.

 - Demonstrated success managing executive-level relationships, including regular engagement with C-suite stakeholders.

 - Experience developing and executing customer success strategies that improve adoption, retention, and customer satisfaction.

 - Proven ability to identify customer risks and develop proactive mitigation plans.

 - Strong written, verbal, and presentation skills with the ability to communicate effectively across technical and executive audiences.

 - Experience collaborating across Sales, Product, Support, and other cross-functional teams to deliver successful customer outcomes.

 - Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple client priorities simultaneously.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience carrying a sales quota or partnering closely with account expansion initiatives.

 - Project management experience.

 - Experience working in a high-growth or startup environment.

 - Experience mentoring Client Success or Account Management team members.

 - Experience designing or improving Customer Success processes, playbooks, or operational programs.




DESCRIPTION

As a Senior Client Success Manager, you will serve as a trusted advisor to a portfolio of enterprise clients, ensuring they realize measurable value from their investment in Strider’s platform and services.


CLIENT SUCCESS & RELATIONSHIP MANAGEMENT

 - Lead the onboarding experience for new enterprise customers, ensuring a seamless transition from Sales and rapid time-to-value.

 - Develop and execute strategic engagement plans that drive product adoption, customer satisfaction, and long-term success.

 - Build trusted relationships with executive sponsors, champions, and key stakeholders across client organizations.

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for strategic customer needs, coordinating internal resources to resolve issues and deliver successful outcomes.

 - Conduct regular business reviews that measure progress against client objectives, identify new opportunities, and strengthen partnerships.


GROWTH & RETENTION

 - Monitor customer health, usage trends, and adoption metrics to proactively identify risks and expansion opportunities.

 - Partner closely with Sales to support renewals, identify cross-sell and upsell opportunities, and contribute to strong net revenue retention.

 - Lead annual contract renewal activities while maintaining a consultative, value-focused approach.


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL LEADERSHIP

 - Share customer insights with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Leadership teams to influence product improvements and enhance the client experience.

 - Contribute to the development of scalable Client Success processes, tools, and best

source_jobs.title: Public Sector Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.


SUMMARY

As a Public Sector Account Executive on Strider’s Strategic Solutions Team, you will play a central role in expanding Strider’s partnerships across the U.S. Government and broader public sector. You will introduce agencies to a new model of connected, AI-powered intelligence—helping them move from fragmented information to mission-ready insight.

This role is ideal for a sales leader who thrives in complex environments, understands the nuances of public sector procurement, and is motivated by meaningful impact. You will own the full sales lifecycle, cultivate trusted relationships with government stakeholders, and collaborate across Strider to deliver intelligence solutions that address evolving national security and economic security priorities.

This is an opportunity to contribute to a high-growth team shaping Strider’s long-term presence in the public sector.




KEY QUALIFICATIONS




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of experience in enterprise software, SaaS, data, or intelligence solution sales.

 - Demonstrated success managing and closing complex sales cycles within U.S. Government or public sector organizations.

 - Experience navigating federal procurement processes, including RFP responses, contract vehicles, COTR engagement, and budget cycles.

 - Proven ability to build relationships with multiple stakeholder groups, including program managers, contracting officers, technical evaluators, and senior executives.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills, including experience delivering executive-level briefings and proposals.

 - Experience using CRM platforms (e.g., Salesforce) to manage pipeline forecasting and account strategy.

 - Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience selling security, risk, intelligence, or analytics platforms.

 - Familiarity with mission sets related to national security, economic security, supply chain resilience, or research protection.

 - Experience supporting agencies within the defense, intelligence, or civilian federal communities.

 - Active or prior U.S. security clearance.




DESCRIPTION

The Public Sector Account Executive will drive revenue growth and strategic account development within assigned public sector territories.




BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT & TERRITORY STRATEGY

 - Develop and execute account strategies to introduce Strider’s intelligence platform to new public sector clients.

 - Identify and prioritize mission-aligned opportunities within federal, defense, and civilian agencies.

 - Map account stakeholders and decision pathways to inform engagement strategy.

 - Maintain accurate forecasting and pipeline management.




SALES LIFECYCLE OWNERSHIP

 - Own the full sales lifecycle—from prospecting and discovery through proposal, negotiation, and close.

 - Lead consultative discovery conversations to understand agency challenges related to geopolitical risk, innovation protection, and supply chain exposure.

 - Partner with internal subject matter experts to deliver tailored product demonstrations and executive briefings.

 - Guide opportunities through procurement channels, including contract vehicles and competitive bidding processes.




STAKEHOLDER ENGAGEMENT

 - Build trusted, long-term relationships with program offices, COTRs, contracting personnel, and executive sponsors.

 - Align Strider’s value proposition to mission priorities and evolving policy landscapes.

 - Represent Strider with professionalism and credibility in government-facing engagements.




CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLLABORATION

 - Partner closely with Product, Engineering,

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.



Job Description:
As a Sales Development Representative on Strider’s Strategic Solutions Team, your job is to introduce Strider’s product offerings to potential new clients within a targeted prospect list that will ultimately drive profitable revenue growth. We’re looking for someone that is intellectually curious and seen as a strategic partner/advisor that first seeks to understand and then adds value.


Responsibilities:

 - Follow automated and standardized processes to qualify and convert a high volume of marketing leads into prospective customers and generate sales-ready meetings for Account Executives.

 - Build and nurture relationships with security executives across enterprise and emerging companies developing highly targeted technology.

 - Execute personalized, value-driven outbound outreach (calls, email, LinkedIn) to generate new opportunities.

 - Conduct thorough discovery and qualification to deliver high-quality, sales-ready opportunities.

 - Maintain accurate CRM data and ensure strong pipeline visibility and hygiene.

 - Meet and exceed quota, contributing to overall revenue growth.

 - Support administrative and operational tasks to enable overall sales team success.

Qualifications:

 - Proven ability (or strong demonstrated potential) to meet or exceed performance targets in a fast-paced environment.

 - Strong, creative, and assertive communication skills—both written and verbal.

 - Ability to engage confidently with senior stakeholders.

 - Hard-working, self-motivated, and driven to achieve excellence.

 - Highly coachable with an openness to feedback and continuous development.

Learning Objectives:

 - Gain hands-on experience with industry-leading sales tools such as Salesforce, SalesLoft, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, and ZoomInfo.

 - Build expertise in prospecting best practices, account prioritization, and time management.

 - Develop a strong understanding of Strider’s sales methodology, MEDDPICC, and how it drives successful deal execution.

Benefits:

 - Competitive Compensation

 - Company Equity Options

 - Flexible PTO in addition to statutory annual leave

 - Two paid company breaks

 - Wellness Reimbursement

 - UK Statutory Holidays (Office Closed)

 - Paid Parental Leave

 - Pension Scheme with employer contributions

 - Comprehensive Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance

Strider is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace and do not discriminate against employees or applicants based on race, color, religion, gender, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We comply with all relevant employment laws in the locations where we operate. This commitment applies to all aspects of employment, including recruitment, hiring, promotion, compensation, and professional development.

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Analyst - Government
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.

Strider is seeking a mission-driven Client Success Manager to lead customer engagement and execution in dynamic, high-stakes environments. As a key member of our customer-facing team, you’ll bridge the gap between Strider’s platform and real-world mission requirements—embedding with government customers, organizing the people, technology, and decisions required to deliver, and serving as the trusted partner who turns mission needs into shipped outcomes. This is a unique opportunity to directly impact national security while helping shape Strider’s future product and customer playbook. If you thrive on driving execution, building trust with operators, and making yourself indispensable to a sophisticated customer, we want to hear from you.



Key Qualifications

· 5+ years of professional experience in customer-facing strategy, program management, product management, solutions, or operator roles—with a track record of delivering outcomes for sophisticated end users.

· Demonstrated ability to own multi-stakeholder programs end-to-end and drive them to measurable, defensible results.

· Experience working with or inside government, defense, intelligence, or national-security organizations; comfort operating in cleared or operational environments.

· Strong product, design, and project-management instincts—you think in terms of users, workflows, and adoption, not features.

· Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to engage both technical and non-technical stakeholders, up to and including senior government leadership.

· Demonstrated ability to operate effectively in ambiguous or evolving mission contexts.

· Willingness to travel 25–50% of the time based on mission requirements.

· Active U.S. security clearance (TS/SCI) or eligibility to obtain one strongly preferred.



Description

As a Client Success Manager, you will be on the front lines of customer delivery—partnering directly with operators, analysts, and program leadership to make Strider an indispensable part of their intelligence fabric. Your role spans customer engagement, relationship management, project management, product management, design thinking, and strategy. It requires high judgment, situational awareness, and relentless ownership of customer outcomes.

You will:

· Own one or more government customer accounts end-to-end, building genuine trust with mission leads, end users, and program leadership.

· Translate ambiguous mission needs into a sequenced plan of work—deciding what to build, what to deprioritize, and when to stop.

· Drive ruthless execution: run the cadences, unblock teams, and hold the line on commitments to the customer.

· Be the product voice in the room—shape requirements with the customer, validate prototypes, and feed structured product signal back to Strider’s platform team so individual deployments compound into product.

· Care about how analysts and operators actually use what we deliver—sweat the workflows, the surfaces, and the moments of friction that decide whether a tool gets adopted or shelved.

· Identify expansion paths inside each account—adjacent missions, new teams, new authorities—and convert proof points into durable, growing relationships.

· Represent the customer internally with clarity and conviction—advocate for what matters; push back on what doesn’t.



Why Join Strider?

· Be a key contributor to a high-impact mission that directly supports innovation and national resilience.

· Join a collaborative, fast-paced team that values creativity, autonomy, and trust.

· Work across domains—engineering, intelligence, product—on some of today’s m

source_jobs.title: Intelligence Advisor (Japan)/インテリジェンス・アドバイザー（日本市場担当）
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.



As an Intelligence Advisor focused on the Japan market, you will help organizations navigate the intersection of geopolitics, emerging technology, and economic security. This role is ideal for someone who can transform complex intelligence and fragmented data into clear, decision-ready insights for executives and operational leaders.

You will work closely with clients in Japan while collaborating across Strider’s intelligence, product, and engineering teams in the United States. Your work will directly inform strategic decisions related to technology competition, supply chain resilience, and national security risk in a rapidly evolving global landscape.




KEY QUALIFICATIONS


MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years professional experience in strategic intelligence, geopolitical risk analysis, economic security, or a related field.

 - Expertise in the People’s Republic of China’s science and technology strategy, including technology acquisition and industrial policy.

 - Experience conducting open-source intelligence (OSINT) research and analysis.

 - Professional proficiency in Mandarin Chinese.

 - Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, including experience briefing senior executive, government, or institutional audiences.

 - Ability to communicate complex analytical findings clearly and effectively to stakeholders in Japanese.

 - Experience developing intelligence products that translate technical or geopolitical developments into operationally relevant insights.

 - Ability to work independently in a fast-paced, client-facing environment with strong ownership and accountability.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience supporting clients or stakeholders in Japan or the broader Asia-Pacific region.

 - Familiarity with Japan’s national security, technology, academic, or industrial landscape.

 - Experience working with large datasets, data analysis tools, or AI-enabled research workflows.

 - Experience collaborating across cross-functional teams including intelligence, product, engineering, or client success organizations.




DESCRIPTION

As an Intelligence Advisor at Strider, you will support Japan-based clients by delivering strategic intelligence that informs executive and operational decision-making. You will analyze geopolitical and technological developments, identify emerging risks, and help organizations better understand the global dynamics shaping innovation and economic competition.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Conduct research and analysis on nation-state activity, with a focus on strategic intent, economic policy, and technology acquisition.

 - Analyze emerging and dual-use technologies, including artificial intelligence, semiconductors, quantum technologies, biotechnology, space systems, and advanced manufacturing.

 - Identify indicators of economic coercion, espionage, illicit technology transfer, and supply chain vulnerabilities.

 - Develop intelligence products that connect technical signals and geopolitical developments to strategic business and operational implications.

 - Map relationships among governments, companies, academic institutions, and global supply chains to identify potential risks and exposures.

 - Deliver timely, actionable insights to senior leaders and operational stakeholders.

 - Serve as a key liaison between Japan-based client needs and U.S.-based intelligence, product, and engineering teams.

 - Identify opportunities to improve Strider’s datasets, workflows, and analytic capabilities for the Japan market.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to adapt products and intelligence capabilities to regional

source_jobs.title: AI Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.


SUMMARY

Strider Technologies is on a mission to deliver strategic intelligence that enables faster, more confident decision-making in an increasingly complex global environment. As an AI Strategy Product Manager, you will define how agentic AI transforms the way users interact with intelligence—turning fragmented data into intuitive, explainable, and actionable insights.

This role sits at the forefront of applied AI innovation. You will lead the strategy and execution of AI-powered capabilities across Strider’s platform, working closely with Engineering, Intelligence, and Design to build products that integrate large language models (LLMs), retrieval systems, and user-facing AI workflows. You will play a critical role in shaping how AI augments human analysis and decision-making at scale.

 


KEY QUALIFICATIONS

Minimum Qualifications

 - 8+ years of experience in product management, product strategy, or related roles, including ownership of complex technical products

 - Experience building or scaling agentic products, including prompt-driven or retrieval-based systems

 - Experience partnering with engineering teams on AI system design, including APIs, model integration, and data workflows

 - Proven ability to define product strategy and deliver end-to-end user-facing AI features

 - Strong analytical skills with experience using data to evaluate product and model performance

 - Ability to translate complex AI concepts into intuitive user experiences and clear business value

 - API Integrations

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience with agentic search

 - Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks, and human-in-the-loop systems

 - Experience building AI products in a SaaS or data platform environment

 - Background in engineering, data science, or applied AI

 - Familiarity with geopolitical risk, intelligence workflows, or analyst-facing tools

 


DESCRIPTION


AI PRODUCT STRATEGY & VISION

 - Define and drive the strategy for agentic AI capabilities across Strider’s platform

 - Identify opportunities to apply agentic workflows to enhance analysis, discovery, and decision-making

 - Translate company objectives into a clear roadmap for AI-driven features, capabilities, and experiences


AI PRODUCT OWNERSHIP & EXECUTION

 - Own the end-to-end lifecycle of agentic features, from concept and experimentation through production deployment

 - Define product requirements, success metrics, and evaluation approaches for AI-powered capabilities

 - Balance rapid iteration with reliability, accuracy, and user trust


AI SYSTEMS & CROSS-FUNCTIONAL LEADERSHIP

 - Partner with Engineering to design and deliver scalable AI systems, including model integration and data pipelines

 - Collaborate with Intelligence teams to ensure AI outputs align with real-world analytical workflows

 - Work with Design to create intuitive, transparent, and controllable AI user experiences


EXPERIMENTATION, EVALUATION & ITERATION

 - Lead experimentation efforts, including prompt iteration, model selection, and UX testing

 - Establish frameworks for evaluating AI outputs (quality, accuracy, usefulness, trust)

 - Continuously refine product direction based on user feedback and model performance


RESPONSIBLE AI & USER TRUST

 - Ensure AI systems are designed with transparency, reliability, and appropriate human oversight

 - Identify and mitigate risks related to bias, hallucination, and misuse

 - Help define best practices for responsible AI use within Strider’s platform


ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

This role will define how generative AI is embedded into Strider’s platform and wor

source_jobs.title: Senior Technical and Corporate Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.

Strider is seeking a Senior Talent Acquisition Partner to help scale hiring across technical and corporate functions during a period of rapid growth. This role will play a critical part in building high-performing teams across engineering, product, operations, finance, marketing, people operations, and other business functions that support Strider’s mission and long-term growth.

As a key member of the People Operations team, you will lead full-cycle recruiting efforts, partner closely with hiring managers and senior leaders, and help evolve recruiting strategies, processes, and candidate experience initiatives across the organization. This role combines hands-on recruiting execution with operational and strategic talent acquisition responsibilities, making it ideal for someone who enjoys building scalable hiring practices in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment.

The ideal candidate is an experienced recruiter who can navigate multiple hiring priorities, build strong partnerships across teams, and help create thoughtful, inclusive hiring processes that support Strider’s continued growth.

 


KEY QUALIFICATIONS


MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 6+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience across technical and corporate functions

 - Experience recruiting for roles across engineering, product, operations, finance, marketing, HR, or other business functions

 - Proven ability to manage multiple searches and stakeholders in a fast-paced, high-growth environment

 - Strong sourcing and talent engagement skills, including experience identifying and attracting passive candidates

 - Experience partnering with hiring managers and leadership teams to define hiring strategies and candidate success profiles

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to build trust across teams and candidates

 - Experience using applicant tracking systems such as Lever, Greenhouse, or Ashby

 - Ability to analyze recruiting data and use insights to improve hiring processes and outcomes

 - Demonstrated commitment to inclusive hiring practices and delivering an exceptional candidate experience

 - Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with professionalism and discretion

 


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience recruiting within technology, SaaS, AI, cybersecurity, or other high-growth industries

 - Familiarity with employer branding initiatives and recruitment marketing strategies

 - Experience supporting hiring in rapidly scaling or globally distributed organizations

 - Experience contributing to recruiting operations, interview process design, or talent acquisition strategy initiatives

 - Experience partnering with cross-functional business leaders on workforce planning and hiring prioritization

 


DESCRIPTION


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Lead full-cycle recruitment across technical and corporate functions, ensuring a seamless and high-quality experience for candidates and hiring teams

 - Partner with hiring managers and business leaders to define hiring needs, align on candidate profiles, and develop effective sourcing and interview strategies

 - Build and execute proactive sourcing strategies through referrals, networking, outreach campaigns, recruiting platforms, and talent communities

 - Develop strong candidate relationships by communicating Strider’s mission, culture, and growth opportunities

 - Serve as a trusted advisor to hiring teams by providing market insights, recruiting trends, and recommendations that support informed hiring decisions

 - Maintain accurate recruiting data, pipeline tracking, and reporting within the applicant tracking sys

source_jobs.title: Staff AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.

We are looking for an exceptionally motivated Staff AI Engineer to join Strider’s Context Engineering team. Context Engineering exists to help shape, stage, and prioritize data for consumption by our analysts, customers, and agentic analytical production processes. We are looking for candidates who are comfortable working across our full data processing pipeline and are motivated by solving problems and having mission impact. We want individuals who are excited to use AI to amplify their leverage and are enthusiastic about the evolving practice of software and agentic engineering.



You will:

 - Build and own services and features to support our Context Engineering organization, focusing on hybrid data search capabilities (using ElasticSearch and S3Vectors) at million to billion document scale.

 - Optimize a variety of LLM-based data processing tasks: for example, entity disambiguation, document quality and relevance scoring, text extraction tasks.

 - Design technical solutions and provide feedback on design documents and architectural decisions across the organization.

 - Participate in code reviews and help improve practices for AI-assisted code review.

What you need to be successful:

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field

 - 10+ years of experience

 - Track record of setting technical direction and executing beyond the small team level.

 - Demonstrated examples of making architectural/technical bets that paid off, or course-corrected when they didn’t.

 - Experience shipping features and solutions that use LLMs in data heavy environments. Experience with frontier model SDKs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.), open-source model providers and frameworks, agentic frameworks.

 - Ability to communicate complex technical concepts and engage with senior stakeholders across disciplines (engineering, product, executives).

 - Self-motivated with excellent problem-solving skills and a strong attention to detail.



Nice-to-haves:

 - Strong opinions about best practices for agentic engineering and AI coding tools, and enthusiasm for further developing best practices.

 - Experience mentoring engineers and developing communities of practice within engineering groups.

 - Comfort and competence deploying features into AWS cloud infrastructure.

 - Master’s degree or PhD in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field



How We Work

We’re a hybrid team that values autonomy, mission impact, and craftsmanship in equal measure. Here’s what that looks like in practice:

 - We own our work end-to-end. Engineers here don’t just write code — they help shape the problem, design the solution, and care about what happens after it ships. You’ll have genuine ownership over meaningful ML systems, not just tickets in a queue.

 - We build with rigor and move with purpose. We believe quality and velocity aren’t at odds. We are looking for developers fully leaning into agentic engineering and AI coding tools to increase their velocity as well as their quality.

 - We collaborate openly across disciplines. Our ML engineers work closely with software engineers and product stakeholders. Perspectives from across the org sharpen our thinking — you’ll both contribute to and learn from those conversations regularly.

 - We make space to go deep. Novel research and creative exploration are encouraged, not squeezed into the margins. We create time for engineers to dig into hard problems and invest in their craft — whether that’s a new modeling approach, a better evaluation framework, or contributing to internal tooling that raises the whole team’s ceiling.

 - We trust each ot

source_jobs.title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.

We’re looking for a Senior Machine Learning Engineer to help Strider unlock value from large-scale document collections. In this role, you’ll build production-ready AI/ML systems for document classification, prioritization, and other document-processing workflows. Your work will directly support the development of scalable intelligence products that help users find meaningful signals in complex, high-volume data.

You’ll work with real-world data and collaborate closely with intelligence and engineering teams to turn complex multilingual information into actionable insights. This role is a strong fit for someone who enjoys technical ownership, builds maintainable systems, and excels at translating ambiguity into measurable product impact.

You will:

 - Design, build, and maintain scalable machine learning solutions, typically focused on document classification tasks using AI/ML models.

 - Work across the full engineering lifecycle from exploratory analysis and prototype development through production deployment, monitoring, iteration, and operational ownership.

 - Take models from R&D into production and deploy them using AWS cloud services.

 - Ensure the reliability and performance of machine learning applications by carrying out continuous testing and optimization.

 - Use AI coding tools such as Cursor and Claude to improve development velocity while maintaining high standards for code quality, reliability, and maintainability.

 - Participate in design reviews, code reviews, and team discussions, providing technical leadership and insight.



What you need to be successful:

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

 - 5+ years of experience in Machine Learning or AI.

 - Strong Python skills and sound software engineering judgment, with the ability to write maintainable, production-oriented code.

 - Experience working with large, complex datasets to assess quality, coverage, value, and tradeoffs.

 - Experience deploying and operating ML or data-processing pipelines in production environments using AWS.

 - Strong understanding of NLP techniques such as tokenization, entity extraction, disambiguation, and language models.

 - Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to engineering partners, product stakeholders, and leadership.

 - Self-motivated, pragmatic, and impact-oriented, with strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.

Nice-to-haves:

 - Strong AWS skills.

 - Experience with Elasticsearch.

 - Experience working with NLP in foreign (non-English) languages.

 - Familiarity with MLOps tools and practices.

 - Experience with PyTorch or Scikit-Learn.

 - Master’s degree or PhD in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field.





How We Work

We’re a hybrid team that values autonomy, mission impact, and craftsmanship in equal measure. Here’s what that looks like in practice:

 - We own our work end-to-end. Engineers here help shape the problem, design the solution, and think carefully about where technical effort can create the most value. Many of our highest-impact opportunities are ambiguous at the start, so we look for people who can make thoughtful decisions, communicate clearly, and adapt as new information emerges.

 - We build with rigor and move with purpose. We believe quality and velocity aren’t at odds. We are looking for developers fully leaning into agentic engineering and AI coding tools to increase their velocity as well as their quality.

 - We collaborate openly across disciplines. Our ML engineers work closely with software engineers, intel specialists, and

source_jobs.title: Data Science Manager, Market Delivery
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Strider is looking for an Applied Intelligence Manager to own North America Market Delivery — the function that sits at the intersection of data science, product, and commercial outcomes. This is not a traditional market-facing role. It is a builder's role. The person who succeeds here has deep technical fluency in data and AI systems, sharp commercial instincts, and the judgment to distinguish custom work that drives strategic value from work that does not.

Market Delivery is an extension of the product organization. This team does not advise, consult, or manage delivery from a distance. They build working solutions alongside Engineering, Growth, and CS — solutions that generate direct ARR, feed the product roadmap, or accelerate the commercial motion in government and enterprise accounts.

North America is a mature market for Strider. The product largely exists, the client base is established, and the bar for custom work is high. The work here is at the edges — extending what is already built, executing high-value engagements for strategic accounts, and ensuring the intelligence flowing back from clients is shaping what we build next.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

Own the North America commercial delivery motion. Partner closely with Growth and CS to identify where custom work creates outsized commercial value — contract expansions, Gov engagements, strategic new logos — and execute it. This team is accountable to measurable ARR contribution, not just activity.

Build working solutions on top of Strider's data infrastructure. Apply data science and AI methods to extend Strider's products for client-specific use cases. That includes entity resolution, risk scoring models, knowledge graph enrichment, and AI-augmented workflows. You are building on the platform, not around it.

Support pre-sale POCs and custom technical engagements. Bring the technical depth clients need to trust that Strider can solve their specific problem. You are the person in the room who can go from a client brief to a working prototype to a commercial outcome.

Feed signal back to the product team. What you learn in client conversations, deployments, and custom engagements is only valuable if it reaches the PMs in a form they can act on. Translating market signal into actionable product requirements is a core part of the job.

Own government delivery alongside the Gov team. Work as an integrated execution partner on government engagements — not independently. Understand the platform deeply enough to build solutions that are reusable across Gov opportunities rather than one-off.

Partner cross-functionally across Product, Engineering, Growth, and CS. This role sits at the seam of multiple functions. The ability to operate credibly in a client conversation, a technical architecture discussion, and a roadmap review in the same week is the job.




WHAT YOU WILL NEED TO BE SUCCESSFUL

 - 5+ years of hands-on data science or applied AI experience, including production-grade model development and deployment.

 - Demonstrated success applying data science methods to commercial problems — entity resolution, risk modeling, knowledge graphs, or similar domains.

 - Deep expertise in Python. Familiarity with search infrastructure (Elasticsearch), graph-based methods, or distributed data frameworks (Spark, Ray) is a strong plus.

 - The ability to move between technical work and client-facing conversations without losing credibility in either. You can explain what you built, why you built it that way, and what it means for the client's problem.

 - Commercial fluency. You understand what makes a client engagem

source_jobs.title: Client Success Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.

Job Summary

We’re seeking a Client Success Operations Manager to build the systems, processes, and insights that power Strider’s client success organization. You’ll own the operational backbone of how we manage the client lifecycle, ensuring our CS team has the data, tooling, and workflows to deliver exceptional client outcomes at scale. You’ll put AI to work across the function, using it to automate routine operations, accelerate insight generation, and multiply what a lean CS org can accomplish. As Strider grows globally, you’ll turn operational complexity into clarity, helping leadership see around corners and helping CSMs spend more time with clients delivering value and less time on process.

You’ll be part of the RevOps Team, which is the engine that helps power our Sales and Client Success teams. RevOps builds the data, systems, and operating cadences that turn Strider’s go-to-market engine into something predictable and scalable, giving leadership a single source of truth and giving every client-facing team the leverage to move faster. It’s a lean, high-impact team where the work you do is visible from day one, where AI is core to how we operate, and where the systems you build directly shape how Strider serves prospects and clients as we grow.

If you’re energized by designing systems that scale, finding the signal in data, and enabling teams to do their best work, this role offers the chance to grow a critical function at Strider.



Key Qualifications

 - 1-3 years of experience in customer success operations, sales operations, or customer success (e.g., CSM), ideally in B2B SaaS.

 - Proven success designing and implementing client lifecycle processes (adoption, risk escalation, renewal, expansion) adopted across a team.

 - Strong experience administering, building, and optimizing CRM and CS tooling (e.g., Salesforce), including workflow automation and data hygiene.

 - Demonstrated ability to build reporting and dashboards on retention and growth metrics (NRR/GRR, logo retention, renewal pipeline, expansion pipeline, health scores, churn risk) and translate them into action.

 - Experience managing renewal forecasting processes and supporting revenue leadership with accurate, timely pipeline visibility.

 - Hands-on experience applying AI tools (e.g., LLM assistants, AI agents, workflow automation, ideally in Claude) to operational work, while maintaining the judgment to know where AI adds leverage versus risk.

 - Excellent communication and relationship-building skills across functions including Client Success, Sales, Finance, and Product.

 - Ability to manage competing priorities and execute with precision in a fast-moving environment.

Preferred: experience with SQL or BI tools (e.g., Databricks, DOMO); experience building AI-assisted reporting or agentic workflows; familiarity with capacity and compensation planning for CS teams; experience supporting clients in regulated or security-conscious industries.



Description

As the Client Success Operations Manager, you’ll own the engine that helps the CS organization runs day to day; this includes its data, systems, processes, and cadences. This is an individual contributor role with broad cross-functional reach.

You’ll build and maintain the reporting layer leadership relies on: renewal pipeline, client health, risk signals, and retention performance. You’ll design the operating rhythm (forecast calls, risk reviews, churn root cause analysis reviews) and the workflows that keep accounts moving through the lifecycle consistently. You’ll support and improve the CS-related tech stack, partnering with internal data

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Software Engineer / シニア フルスタック ソフトウェアエンジニア
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.

Strider is seeking a mission-driven Full Stack Software Engineer to deliver cutting-edge technical solutions in a dynamic global environment. You’ll work across two intersecting areas: building the intelligent data pipelines focused on the APAC region and collaborating with Strider’s global engineering team to create custom features that bring those capabilities to analysts and customers.

This role is a fit for someone who is energized by working close to AI systems, comfortable across a full stack of software development, and excited to solve complex problems in a collaborative, global team environment.



What You’ll Do

 - Serve as Strider’s technical lead in Japan, provide critical market insights and collaborate closely with engineers, product managers, and analysts to design and deliver scalable, well-crafted solutions.

 - Build and own features across the full stack.

 - Work with analysts, clients and business partners to create custom solutions using Strider’s proprietary data assets.

 - Work with frontier model SDKs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) and open-source frameworks to ship AI-powered features into production.

 - Investigate and prototype solutions to complex system design challenges and translate findings into maintainable, production-ready code.
   
   

What You Need to Be Successful

 - 5+ years of experience in software engineering

 - Bilingual Japanese and English speaker

 - Experience building full-stack applications

 - Experience and agentic coding processes with LLMs or AI/ML systems in a production or project context

 - Proficiency in Python, Java, or similar languages

 - Experience with modern front-end frameworks and APIs

 - Strong problem-solving instincts, an ability to ask good questions, and a pragmatic approach to unforeseen challenges

 - Bachelor’s degree in computer science or a related field (or equivalent experience)



Nice to Haves

 - Familiarity with cloud infrastructure (AWS preferred)

 - Experience with databases such as SQL Server, DynamoDB, or Memgraph, and search systems like ElasticSearch

 - Experience with Material UI, Storybook, or unit testing frameworks

 - Experience with project tracking tools like JIRA



How We Work



We own our work end-to-end. Engineers here don’t just write code — they help shape the problem, design the solution, and care about what happens after it ships. You’ll have genuine ownership over meaningful systems, not just tickets in a queue.

We build with rigor and move with purpose. We believe quality and velocity aren’t at odds. We lean into agentic engineering and AI coding tools to increase both.

We collaborate openly across disciplines. Our engineers work closely with product stakeholders and ML practitioners. Perspectives from across the org sharpen our thinking — you’ll both contribute to and learn from those conversations regularly.

We make space to go deep. Creative exploration is encouraged, not squeezed into the margins. We create time for engineers to invest in their craft — whether that’s a better evaluation framework, a new modeling approach, or internal tooling that raises the whole team’s ceiling.

We trust each other with the hard stuff. You’ll be working on problems where the answers aren’t obvious. We want people who are comfortable with ambiguity, willing to surface concerns early, and confident enough to push back when something doesn’t seem right.



Why Join



Immediate Mission Impact — Join a small, agile team where your contributions will have a direct, tangible impact on Strider’s mission from day one.

Data-Rich Environment — Leverage our large, diverse, proprietary dat

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager / シニアプロダクトマネージャー
source_jobs.description:
Strider Technologies delivers strategic intelligence that helps organizations make faster, more confident decisions in an increasingly complex global environment. Using cutting-edge AI and proprietary methodologies, we transform open-source data into actionable insights that help protect technology, talent, and supply chains from nation-state risks.

Job Description

As a Senior Product Manager you will lead the product vision, strategy, and roadmap. You will work closely with our engineering and intelligence team, with emphasis on the delivery component of product management. You will be the product owner of your domain and provide support to the engineering team while delivering cross-functional value to stakeholders.

What you will do:

 - Define Strider’s products, from strategy and solution design to roadmap and delivery plans.

 - Build data-driven products at scale through partnerships with Design and Engineering Teams.

 - Manage the entire product life cycle, from ideation to execution.

 - Operate cross-functionally and build strong relationships within Strider and with key external partners.

 - Foster an environment that enables the team to execute quickly on the right things, while maintaining high quality.

 - Expected to lead the process of making recommendations and setting technical requirements for overall functionality, new initiatives and other enhancements.



What you will need to be successful:

 - 6+ years of experience delivering highly successful and innovative software products as a product manager, product strategist or in a related technical role.

 - Demonstrable experience working with supply chain, firmographic, and/or risk (E.g. sanctions, export control) data/workflow tools

 - Passion for creative, resourceful problem solving.

 - Demonstrated ability to manage multiple projects and teams - work prioritization, planning, and task delegation.

 - Demonstrated ability to coordinate software projects with engineering teams.

 - Bilingual Japanese and English speaker



Nice to haves:

 - Experience in fast-paced start-up SaaS environment

 - Strong understanding/experience with product discovery including qualitative and quantitative analysis, customer and market analysis, and experimenting and testing.

 - Strong understand/experience with product design including hypothesis testing, problem identification, building and scaling MVP based development.

 - Experience working as an engineer or with engineers to support a highly technical product.



Benefits

 - Competitive Compensation

 - Company Equity Options

 - Statutory Health Insurance plus Additional Health Screenings

 - Paid Parental Leave

 - 10+ Days Annual Leave plus National Holidays

 - Wellness Reimbursement

 - Commuter Allowance



職務内容

シニアプロダクトマネージャーとして、プロダクトのビジョン、戦略、ロードマップを主導していただきます。エンジニアリングチームおよびインテリジェンスチームと緊密に連携し、特にプロダクトマネジメントにおけるデリバリー(実行・提供)の側面に重点を置いていただきます。担当領域のプロダクトオーナーとして、エンジニアリングチームをサポートしながら、部門横断的な価値をステークホルダーに提供していただきます。

主な業務内容:

 - 戦略立案、ソリューション設計からロードマップ、デリバリー計画まで、Striderのプロダクトを定義する

 - デザインチームおよびエンジニアリングチームとの連携を通じて、データドリブンなプロダクトをスケールで構築する

 - アイデア創出から実行まで、プロダクトライフサイクル全体を管理する

 - 部門横断的に業務を遂行し、社内および主要な社外パートナーとの強固な関係を構築する

 - チームが高い品質を維持しながら、正しいことに迅速に取り組める環境を醸成する

 - 全体的な機能性、新規イニシアチブ、その他の改善に関する提案および技術要件の設定プロセスを主導することが期待される

成功するために必要な要件:

 - プロダクトマネージャー、プロダクトストラテジスト、または関連する技術職として、優れて革新的なソフトウェアプロダクトを提供してきた6年以上の経験

 - サプライチェーン、ファーモグラフィック(企業属性)データ、および/またはリスク(例:制裁措置、輸出管理)関連のデータ・ワークフローツールに関する実証可能な業務経験

 - 創造的かつ臨機応変な問題解決への情熱

 - 複数のプロジェクトおよびチームを管理する実証された能力(業務の優先順位付け、計画立案、タスクの委任)

 - エンジニアリングチームとソフトウェアプロジェクトを調整する実証された能力

 - バイリンガル(日本語・英語)

歓迎するスキル・経験:

 - スピード感のあるスタートアップ・SaaS環境での経験

 - 定性・定量分析、顧客・市場分析、実験・検証を含むプロダクトディスカバリーに関する高い理解・経験

 - 仮説検証、課題特定、MVP(実用最小限の製品)構築・スケーリングを含むプロダクトデザインに関する高い理解・経験

 - 高度な技術的プロダクトをサポートするため、エンジニアとして、またはエンジニアと協働した経験

Strider is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace and do not discriminate against employees or applicants based on race, color, religion, gen

notes: Strider Technologies is a strategic intelligence company that uses AI and proprietary methodologies to transform open-source data into actionable insights.
